questions.question: What is the primary role of a web browser?
choices: To render HTML into a visual web page
To store website files on a server
questions.explanation: A web browser reads HTML code and renders it into a visual web page that users can see and interact with.

questions.question: In the client-server model, which part requests a web page?
questions.explanation: In the client-server model, the client (typically a web browser) sends a request to the server, which then responds with the web page files.

questions.question: How is HTML compared to building blocks in the Lego analogy?
choices: HTML elements snap together to build a web page, like Lego bricks build a structure
HTML must be purchased in sets like Lego kits
HTML pages break apart easily like Lego pieces
HTML comes with instruction manuals like Lego sets
questions.explanation: HTML elements are like Lego bricks — individual building blocks that snap together to create the structure of a web page.

questions.question: What does a server do when it receives a request for a web page?
choices: It writes new HTML code on the fly
It sends the requested files back to the client
It opens the web page in its own browser
It converts the page into a mobile app
questions.explanation: When a server receives a request, it locates the requested files (HTML, CSS, images, etc.) and sends them back to the client browser.

questions.question: HTML stands for HyperText ___ Language.
questions.explanation: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language. It is a markup language used to structure content on the web.

questions.question: In the client-server model, the web browser acts as the ___.
questions.explanation: The web browser is the client that sends requests to servers and displays the responses as web pages.
